notes: With-skill outputs consistently provided multiple variations (2-3 strategic approaches) with named frameworks, A/B testing suggestions, and audience segment recommendations — this was the primary differentiator.
Without-skill outputs were competent but provided only single versions with no strategic optionality.

The landing-page-b2b-analytics eval showed both configurations performing equally on assertions (6/6), suggesting the assertions may not capture the qualitative depth differences. Human review is important here.
With-skill runs used ~30% more tokens and took ~2.5x longer due to reading the skill + reference files and producing multiple variations. This is an expected tradeoff for richer output.

The 'multiple_variations' assertion was the primary discriminator — it failed for all without_skill runs. Other assertions passed for both configurations, indicating baseline Claude already writes solid copy but lacks the structured multi-variation approach.
